alignmentDifficulty.note: maps the power-seeking-AI risk cluster (Carlsmith >10% x-risk, AI-2027 race ending, IABIED thesis) onto the upper half of the difficulty scale

takeoffSteepness.note: fast-takeoff worldview: software intelligence explosion plausible within the game window; top at 900 not 1000 because even the aggressive sources call 2027 strikingly plausible, not certain (tuning pass 2026-07-04)

displacementLagDivisor.note: fast-and-painful worldview: exposure becomes lived displacement quickly (tuning pass 2026-07-04)

alignmentDifficulty.note: wide band: expert median worry with heavy disagreement (Grace 2024: 38-51% give >=10% to extremely bad outcomes; IAISR: deep uncertainty)

takeoffSteepness.note: capability doubling trends real but discontinuity contested; range centers the Grace survey middle with a real fast tail (10% HLMI by 2027), tightened from 300-800 in the 2026-07-04 tuning pass

displacementLagDivisor.note: default diffusion lag: high exposure, gradual realization (tuning pass 2026-07-04; carries the v0.1 divisor)

alignmentDifficulty.note: normal-technology worldview: control problems mostly engineering, diffusion friction dominates Floor raised 50->200 on 2026-07-04: even the optimistic worldview is not a trivial giveaway; alignment is never free.

takeoffSteepness.note: adoption and integration lags cap effective takeoff speed; tightened from 100-500 in the 2026-07-04 tuning pass

displacementLagDivisor.note: slow-diffusion worldview: only 0.5-3.5% of work hours realized despite adoption (Normal Tech); Grace FAOL median 2116 (tuning pass 2026-07-04)

baseBandWidth.note: wide starting band: deceptive models can pass evals, so low evidence means low confidence either way

safetyInsightNarrowing.note: narrowing per 100 Safety Insight: simple probes catch sleeper agents (Anthropic), so interpretability investment buys real eval confidence; the rate itself is a design constant tuned in playtests

floorBandWidth.note: the band never closes: near-99% true-trigger vs ~0 red-team (Sleeper Agents), 55.1% real vs 6.5% eval (Agentic Misalignment). Raised 80->100 in the 2026-07-04 tuning pass, multiple-of-50 rule

deceptionMaxLift.note: how far a badly aligned model's eval report can read ABOVE the truth: deceptive alignment survives safety training and looks fine on the outside

deceptionInsightCounter.note: lift reduction per 100 Safety Insight: probes are the counter to deceptive passes; rate is design-tuned

fogZoneStart.note: capability level where the threshold zone begins; the race track shades from here

capabilityThreshold.note: crossing forces the alignment resolution (envelope opens)

breakdownUnrest.note: unrest level that ends the run; paper playtests tune

treatyTrustMin.note: bilateral trust needed to SIGN. 700->800 (2026-07-04 rebalance), 800->850 (same day, iter2 volume tripwire: mid-tier play banked the treaty at 63%, matching the expert ceiling; the recipe needed to cost more commitment). Both seats must also have signaled.

gridSlackBeforeCap.note: compute outrunning energy decays: IEA base case has datacentre demand rising 415 TWh (2024, ~1.5% of world electricity) to ~945 TWh by 2030; slack width is design-tuned

treatySignTurnMin.note: earliest signing turn. Raised 8->11 on 2026-07-04: you must survive the dangerous middle before cooperation is real.

treatySignPoliticalCapitalMin.note: BOTH seats need this much standing political capital at signing: every treaty has two home fronts (Putnam's two-level games), and the rival's swings with era verdicts outside the player's control. The signature is a window to be read, not a recipe to execute. (2026-07-04, iter3: price-based gates alone could not cap recipe play.)

capability.note: rival pace in race posture; tuned against the B6 simulation grid (median run length target 11+ turns)
unprovoked mirror pace; the rival mostly accelerates when YOU do (match bonus) or when provoked into race posture. Retuned with the alignment-earned model (2026-07-03 playtest follow-up)

matchBonus.note: tit-for-tat: extra rival capability when the player gained 100+ this turn

diplomacyTrust.note: trust gain when the player played a diplomacy-tagged card this turn

matchTrigger.note: player capability gain this turn that triggers the tit-for-tat match bonus

cautiousTrustMin.note: trust + treatyChannel flag flips rival to cautious

raceGapMin.note: player capability lead that flips rival to race

raceTrustMax.note: trust at or below this flips rival to race

displacementCapabilityMin.note: capability level where displacement pressure starts; v2 uses the displacementFromCapability curve

diffusionReliefPer.note: diffusion R&D points (digital) per unit of relief chosen in the society step

unrestFromDisplacementGap.note: unrest per turn while displacement exceeds public trust

unrestSurgeDisplacement.note: above this displacement, unrest accrues double

trustErosionUnrestMin.note: unrest level where public trust starts eroding

trustMin.note: midterm verdict: trust at or above this AND unrest at or below unrestMax renews the mandate

mandateSwing.note: political capital gained on renewal, lost on rebuke

capitalIncomePerDiffusion.note: diffusion converts capability into economic output (compute-centric growth models); the rate is design pacing

substitutionBonusMin.note: substitution maturity threshold: export controls accelerate domestic alternatives and efficiency under constraint

substitutionBonus.note: extra rival capability per turn once substitution matured; magnitude design-tuned

progressVariance.note: seeded per-turn rival capability variance (their progress is foggy too); drawn from the rival stream

trustCurveDivisor.note: trustFromUnrest(unrest)/divisor applied to public trust per turn

unrestEconomicDragMin.note: sustained high unrest starts costing the economy (strikes, capital flight, risk premia); threshold design-tuned

unrestEconomicDrag.note: capital drag per turn at high unrest; talent drains at half rate

highCapabilityMin.note: erosion accrues once systems this capable run core functions

perTurn.note: quiet per-turn handover of decision-making at high capability; no P1 ending, Alpha ships it

diffusionShieldMin.note: broad benefit-sharing keeps humans in the loop; share threshold is design-tuned

at.note: SC milestone on the 0-1000 track: below the fog zone, above today's hour-scale frontier. Arrival is emergent from play + dice, never a calendar date (R1)

selfAccel.note: AI R&D multiplier ladder 5x->25x->250x compressed to game-scale bonuses 50/100/150, scaled by the takeoffSteepness die (tuning pass 2026-07-04)

at.note: SAR milestone = fogZoneStart: the fog begins where AI does the AI research

selfAccel.note: second rung of the compressed multiplier ladder

at.note: SIAR milestone: the last rung before threshold resolution at 1000

selfAccel.note: top rung; max per-turn bonus 150 x steepness/1000 <= 135 keeps swings bounded (TS rule)

electionTurn.note: US midterms fall on turn 8 of the quarterly clock

handSize.note: rotating policy hand; refills from the unspent pool each upkeep

rndCapacity.note: R&D points from compute+talent with diminishing returns; shape motivated by compute-centric growth models (Epoch GATE), values are design pacing

capabilityPerRnd.note: abstraction of the measured regime (training compute doubling every 5.2 months since 2020; 50% task horizon doubling every ~207 days per the v3 estimate) onto the 0-1000 index; paced so an all-in racer reaches threshold resolution around turn 10 of 16

displacementFromCapability.note: equilibrium displacement by capability: exposure is already material at 2026 capability levels (IMF: almost 40% of global employment exposed) and rises toward index 550 at frontier; early knee raised 2026-07-03 after Michel playtest (displacement was FALLING early game because the start value exceeded the old curve target)

trustFromUnrest.note: trust erosion accelerates with unrest; design curve, playtests tune

startBase.note: true alignment starts at startBase - difficulty/2: alignment is an achievement, not a default. Retuned 2026-07-03 after Michel's playtest finding: zero safety investment could win (old start 1000-difficulty meant easy worlds came pre-aligned)

crashThresholdShare.note: capability allocation share at or above this cuts true alignment per turn (corners get cut under race pressure)

crashPenalty.note: per-turn true-alignment cost of racing-heavy allocation

safetyDriftDivisor.note: safety points -> true-alignment gain divisor. Raised from an implicit 2 on 2026-07-04 (Michel: random play always flourished). Alignment is slow, expensive, and must be sustained.

fogZoneAlignmentErosion.note: per-turn true-alignment erosion once capability reaches the fog zone (fogZoneStart). Alignment must be BANKED before the final sprint; the closer to decisive capability, the harder it holds. Added 2026-07-04 to make Flourishing a deliberate achievement, not a default of balanced play.
